hero.headline:
Rollers that
never stop.
hero.body: We design, manufacture, and supply conveyor idlers and complete conveyor systems for construction and bulk-material projects across Uzbekistan.

history.headline:
Three Decades Building
Uzbekistan's Conveyor Backbone
history.body: Nam Roller started as a small repair workshop in Namangan, fixing worn-out conveyor rollers for local quarries. Three decades on, we've grown into a full manufacturing facility producing idlers, pulleys, and complete conveyor systems — trusted by cement plants, mining operations, and construction sites nationwide.

milestones.text: Founded in Namangan as a small workshop repairing conveyor rollers for local quarries.
Opened our own manufacturing facility, moving from repair work to in-house production.
Over 1,000 projects delivered, with systems running at up to 12,000 t/day nationwide.

projects.name: Cement Plant Conveyor Line
projects.text: Designed, manufactured, and installed a complete trough-idler conveyor line rated for 8,000 t/day of clinker.

projects.name: Quarry Processing Upgrade
projects.text: Replaced an aging idler fleet with heavy-duty impact rollers and drive pulleys, cutting unplanned downtime.

cog.p: Tube cutting, turning, welding, and assembly under one roof in Namangan.

shield.p: Sealed 2Z bearings and St37 steel tubes engineered for a long service life.

ruler.p: Standard diameters in stock; custom lengths and trough angles built to order.

truck.p: Delivery across Uzbekistan with on-site installation and commissioning.

capPage.headline: We Manufacture Rollers and Conveyors
capPage.body: Our production process for rollers and conveyors — from cutting, welding, and assembly through to testing.

preview.headline: Rollers for Every Conveyor
preview.body: A full range of idlers, pulleys, and conveyor systems — in stock and made to order for any bulk-material or construction application.

cta.headline: Need Rollers Urgently?
cta.body: Standard sizes are in stock. Tell us your belt width — we'll quote a price within 48 hours.

compare.full: You can compare up to 4 items
compare.title: Product Comparison
compare.quoteNote: Please quote a price for the following items:

items.a: Standard idlers and pulleys are usually in stock and ship within a few days. Custom items and complete systems are manufactured within 2–4 weeks, depending on volume and complexity.

items.q: Is there a minimum order quantity?
items.a: There is no strict minimum. We handle everything from single replacement items to large batches for new conveyor lines. Tell us your requirements and we'll recommend the best option.

items.q: Do you provide delivery and installation?
items.a: Yes. We deliver our products across Uzbekistan, and for complete systems our own crew handles installation, supervision, and commissioning on site.
Yes. We manufacture idlers in custom lengths, diameters, and trough angles, as well as pulleys built to the specific parameters of your belt and frame.

items.q: What warranty applies to your products?
items.a: All our products carry a quality warranty. We use sealed 2Z bearings and St37 steel tubes engineered for a long service life in dusty and abrasive conditions.

footer.desc: Conveyor idlers and complete conveyor systems, manufactured in Uzbekistan for construction and bulk-material projects.
footer.contactTitle: Have questions? Get in touch with us.

cookieBanner.label: Cookies
cookieBanner.text: We use only essential cookies — to remember your language and protect forms from spam. There are no advertising or tracking cookies.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Policy describes what data Nam Roller collects through the namroller.uz website, how we use it, and how we protect it. By using the site, you agree to the terms below.
privacy.contactNote: For questions about your data, write to

sections.title: What Data We Collect
body: When you submit a quote request or message through the forms on our site, we receive the information you provide: name, phone number, email address, company name, and the content of your message.

In addition, our server automatically records technical data — IP address, browser type, and visit time — for security and to ensure the site operates reliably. We do not use tracking tools for advertising purposes.

sections.title: How We Use Your Data
body: The data we receive is used only to respond to your inquiry, prepare a commercial proposal, arrange delivery, and improve the operation of the site.
We do not send marketing communications without your consent and do not use your contact details for purposes unrelated to your request.

sections.title: Sharing with Third Parties
body: We do not sell or share your data with third parties for commercial purposes.

Data is processed only through services necessary for the site to function: secure storage (Supabase), spam protection for forms (Cloudflare Turnstile), and sales team notifications (Telegram). Each of these receives only the minimum data required for its function.

sections.title: Storage and Protection
body: Data is stored on secure servers with restricted access and is used only by a limited number of sales staff.
We retain inquiries for as long as necessary to process the request and meet legal requirements, after which they are deleted.

sections.title: Cookies
body: The site uses a minimal set of cookies: to remember your selected language and to protect forms from automated bots. No advertising or third-party tracking cookies are used.

sections.title: Your Rights
body: You may request access to your data, its correction, or its deletion. To do so, contact us at the email address below and we will process your request within a reasonable timeframe.

sections.title: Changes to This Policy
body: We may update this Policy from time to time. The current version is always published on this page along with the date of the last change.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: These Terms govern the use of the namroller.uz website. Please review them before using the site or submitting an inquiry.
terms.contactNote: For questions about these Terms, write to

sections.title: Acceptance of Terms
body: By using the namroller.uz website, you confirm your agreement with these Terms. If you do not agree with them, please do not use the site.

sections.title: Product Information
body: Technical specifications, images, and product descriptions are provided for reference. Exact parameters, materials, and configuration are confirmed in writing when an order is placed.
We reserve the right to change the catalog, specifications, and product availability without prior notice.

sections.title: Inquiries and Commercial Proposals
body: Submitting a quote request or inquiry form constitutes a request for consultation, not the conclusion of a contract. A commercial proposal is valid for the period stated in it.
Obligations between the parties arise only upon signing a separate supply agreement.

sections.title: Intellectual Property
body: The content of the site — text, logo, images, graphics, and design — belongs to Nam Roller. Use of these materials without written permission is not permitted.

sections.title: Acceptable Use
body: You agree not to use the site for unlawful purposes, not to disrupt its operation, not to attempt unauthorized access, and not to use automated tools to collect data without permission.

sections.title: Limitation of Liability
body: The site is provided "as is." We strive to maintain the accuracy of information but do not guarantee it is error-free and are not liable for indirect losses arising from use of the site.
The site may contain links to external resources, for the content of which we are not responsible.

sections.title: Governing Law
body: These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Uzbekistan. Disputes are resolved in accordance with applicable law.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: This page explains which cookies the namroller.uz website uses and why. We use only what is necessary for the site to function.
cookies.contactNote: For questions about cookies, write to

sections.title: What Are Cookies
body: Cookies are small text files that a website stores in your browser. They help the site remember your settings and function correctly on subsequent visits.

sections.title: Which Cookies We Use
body: Language settings — remember your selected language (Russian or Uzbek) so the site opens in the correct version.
Spam protection — Cloudflare Turnstile uses a temporary technical cookie to distinguish humans from automated bots when submitting forms.
We do not use advertising, marketing, or third-party analytics cookies.

sections.title: Consent and Management
body: The cookies we use are strictly necessary for the site to function and are applied to support basic functionality.
You can delete or block cookies in your browser settings. Note that doing so may cause some features — such as remembering your language and form protection — to work incorrectly.

sections.title: Third-Party Services
body: Form protection is provided by Cloudflare Turnstile, which may set its own temporary cookie. Its use is governed by Cloudflare's privacy policy.

sections.title: Changes
body: We may update this Cookie Policy. The current version is always published on this page along with the date of the last change.

trough-idler.name: Trough Idler
trough-idler.blurb: A three-roll set for transporting bulk materials on inclined and horizontal conveyors.
trough-idler.overview: The trough idler is a three-roll set that shapes the belt into a trough to increase the volume of material transported. The side rolls are set at a 20–35° angle, which keeps bulk material centered on the belt and prevents spillage on inclined and horizontal conveyors. It is the fundamental load-bearing component of any quarry or construction conveyor line.

process.title: Tube Cutting
process.text: Seamless St37 tube is cut to length and faced for bearing housing fitment.

process.title: Turning
process.text: Shaft ends and bearing housings are turned to a precise fit, ensuring roll alignment.

process.title: Welding and Assembly
process.text: Housings are welded into the tube, and sealed 2Z bearings and labyrinth seals are pressed in.

process.title: Quality Control
process.text: Each roller is checked for runout, ease of rotation, and sealing before assembly into the set.

features.title: Long Service Life
features.text: St37 steel tube and sealed 2Z bearings are built for years of operation in dust and abrasive conditions.

features.title: High Load Capacity
features.text: Up to 1,200 N per set — handles heavy crushed stone and ore on high-throughput lines.

features.title: Trough Angle to Suit the Application
features.text: 20°, 30°, and 35° angles available for different belt widths and material characteristics.

features.title: Smooth Rotation
features.text: Labyrinth seals protect the bearings, reducing resistance and belt wear.

return-roller.name: Return Idler
return-roller.blurb: A single roll support for the return strand of the belt. Available with rubber discs for sticky materials.
return-roller.overview: The return idler supports the lower, return strand of the conveyor belt between the drive and tail pulleys. A single straight roll keeps the belt from sagging and stabilizes its travel. For sticky materials, a version with rubber discs is available that self-cleans and prevents carryback buildup.

process.title: Tube Cutting
process.text: Steel tube is cut to length with a 4–5 mm wall thickness rated for the design load.

process.title: Turning
process.text: Bearing seats and Ø20/Ø25 shaft ends are turned for precise centering.

process.title: Assembly
process.text: 6204 2Z bearings are pressed in; self-cleaning rubber discs are fitted where required.

process.title: Quality Control
process.text: Runout, balance, and free rotation are checked before painting.

features.title: Carryback Protection
features.text: Rubber discs shed sticky material buildup, extending belt service life.

features.title: Stable Belt Travel
features.text: The straight roll eliminates sag on the return strand and reduces vibration.

features.title: Load Capacity up to 900 N
features.text: Rated for long return-strand spans on heavy-duty conveyors.

features.title: Easy Installation
features.text: Standard shaft mounting dimensions simplify replacement in existing frames.

impact-roller.name: Impact Idler
impact-roller.blurb: Rollers with rubber rings that absorb impact loads at transfer and loading points.
impact-roller.overview: The impact idler is installed at loading and transfer points where falling material strikes the belt. A steel core is covered with vulcanized rubber rings of 65 Sh hardness that absorb the impact, protect the belt from puncture, and extend its service life. This is a critical component at points receiving large, heavy loads.

process.title: Core Cutting
process.text: St37 steel tube is cut and machined to form the roller's load-bearing core.

process.title: Ring Vulcanization
process.text: Rubber rings of 65 Sh hardness are vulcanized and fitted onto the core to absorb impact.

process.title: Assembly
process.text: Reinforced 6205/6306 2Z bearings are pressed in, and rings are secured along the full length of the roller.

process.title: Quality Control
process.text: Ring hardness, runout, and impact resistance are checked before shipment.

features.title: Belt Protection
features.text: Rubber rings absorb impact and prevent puncturing of costly belting.

features.title: 2,000 N Impact Load Rating
features.text: Rated for the impact of large lumps at loading points.

features.title: Reinforced Bearings
features.text: 6205/6306 2Z bearings withstand elevated dynamic loads.

features.title: Ring Diameter 133–194 mm
features.text: Multiple sizes available for varying transfer intensity.

guide-roller.name: Self-Aligning / Training Idler
guide-roller.blurb: Self-aligning rollers that correct belt drift and prevent edge wear.
guide-roller.overview: The training idler automatically corrects belt drift, keeping it centered on the conveyor axis. A central swivel assembly reacts to drift, while vertical guide rolls gently return the belt into position. This prevents edge wear, material spillage, and unplanned shutdowns on long conveyor runs.

process.title: Tube Cutting
process.text: Tube is cut to length for the Ø108/133 load-bearing roll.

process.title: Turning
process.text: Bearing seats and the central swivel assembly are turned to fit.

process.title: Assembly
process.text: The swivel frame is assembled and two Ø89 vertical guide rolls are fitted.

process.title: Quality Control
process.text: Swivel freedom, drift response, and smooth rotation are checked.

features.title: Automatic Belt Alignment
features.text: The swivel assembly continuously returns the belt to the conveyor axis.

features.title: Edge Protection
features.text: Vertical rolls prevent abrasion and delamination of the belt edges.

features.title: Less Downtime
features.text: Reduces the risk of belt run-off and unplanned stoppages on long runs.

features.title: Load Capacity up to 1,000 N
features.text: Rated for belt widths of 650–1,200 mm.

drive-pulley.name: Drive and Tail Pulley
drive-pulley.blurb: Lagged and unlagged pulleys for head, tail, and take-up positions.
drive-pulley.overview: Pulleys transfer traction to the belt (drive pulley) and set its tension and direction (tail, take-up). The steel shell is welded to a turned C45 shaft and mounted in SN/SNL plummer block bearings. Smooth or diamond-pattern rubber lagging of 10–14 mm increases grip and prevents slippage under load.

process.title: Shell and Disc Cutting
process.text: Steel plate and tube are cut to form the shell and end discs at the required diameter.

process.title: Shaft Turning
process.text: The C45 shaft is turned, with keyways cut for mounting and coupling.

process.title: Welding, Lagging, and Balancing
process.text: The shell is welded to the shaft, rubber lagging is applied, and the pulley is balanced.

process.title: Quality Control
process.text: Static or dynamic balancing and geometry checks are performed before shipment.

features.title: Diameter 250–630 mm
features.text: A complete size range for head, tail, and take-up positions.

features.title: Reliable Lagging
features.text: Smooth or diamond-pattern rubber, 10–14 mm, improves grip and service life.

features.title: SN/SNL Plummer Block Bearings
features.text: Split housings simplify on-site installation and maintenance.

features.title: Balancing on Request
features.text: Static or dynamic balancing for high belt speeds.

complete-system.name: Complete Conveyor System
complete-system.blurb: Turnkey belt conveyors — frame, idlers, pulleys, drive, and structure, delivered and installed.
complete-system.overview: The complete conveyor system is a turnkey engineering solution: design, engineering, manufacturing, and installation of the entire line. We supply the frame, idler sets, pulleys, drive, and steel structures as a single package sized to your material, throughput, and site geometry — from a single span to a multi-section route.

process.title: Engineering
process.text: Our engineers calculate throughput, incline angle, and drive sizing for your material and site.

process.title: Manufacturing
process.text: The frame, idlers, pulleys, and structures are manufactured at our facility in Namangan.

process.title: Delivery and Installation
process.text: The package is delivered to site and installed by our crew anywhere in Uzbekistan.

process.title: Commissioning
process.text: The line is started up, the belt is aligned, and the system is handed over to the customer for operation.

features.title: Turnkey Delivery
features.text: Engineering, manufacturing, installation, and commissioning from a single source.

features.title: Throughput up to 12,000 t/day
features.text: Sized to match your production volumes.

features.title: Durable Construction
features.text: Galvanized or painted steel for long outdoor service life.

features.title: Installation Across Uzbekistan
features.text: Delivery, on-site supervision, and service anywhere in the country.

projects.headline: Projects Across Uzbekistan
projects.body: From Karakalpakstan to the Fergana Valley — our rollers and conveyors are at work on sites nationwide. Hover over a point to see the project.
projects.hint: Hover or tap a point on the map

process.headline: How We Make Rollers and Conveyors
process.body: A complete cycle under one roof in Namangan — from tube cutting to a finished conveyor system. See how we manufacture rollers and conveyors.

stages.title: Cutting
stages.text: St37 steel tube is cut to length on CNC machines with a length tolerance of ±1 mm.

stages.title: Welding
stages.text: Bushings and flanges are welded to the shell in a jig for precise alignment.

stages.title: Turning
stages.text: Bearing seats are turned on lathes for a tight 2Z fit.

stages.title: Assembly
stages.text: Sealed bearings, shafts, and seals are assembled and packed with lifetime lubricant.

stages.title: Quality Control
stages.text: Every roller is checked for runout, rotation, and load capacity before packing and shipment.

testimonials.headline: What Our Customers Say
testimonials.body: See how our conveyor idlers and systems perform on sites across Uzbekistan.

seed.text: These conveyor idlers have run three seasons on our crushing line without a single bearing failure. Reliable equipment and honest lead times.
NamRoller engineered a 1200 mm idler set for our quarry. Drawings were approved quickly, and build quality was excellent.
The support team walked us through installation step by step. Spare parts arrived within days — exactly what an industrial site needs.
Switching to their rollers noticeably cut our downtime. Easy integration with our existing frames was the deciding factor.
Durable components and fast technical support responses have transformed our material handling process. A very efficient supplier.
The startup exceeded expectations. Tolerances were precise, and the system boosted overall line throughput.
Quality steel rollers, fair pricing, and a team that truly understands bulk materials. Our conveyors run quieter now.
They delivered a turnkey solution that matched our requirements exactly and increased throughput across two plants.
From the quote to commissioning, everything went smoothly. We've already placed a repeat order for a second line.
We ordered heavy-duty rollers for a cement plant. They handle high dust levels and load well, with minimal maintenance.
We've been working together for two years now. Consistent quality batch after batch and responsive technical support.
Rollers were installed on an inclined conveyor — running quietly with no vibration. Recommended as a reliable manufacturer.
